# @json-express/middleware-auth — AI Context

JWT authentication middleware. Verifies `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on generated routes and feeds the verified payload to model `access` rules.

## Install — that is the whole integration
```
npm i @json-express/middleware-auth
```
Discovery is automatic. **Never import it or implement custom JWT verification.**

## Configuration (.env — case-insensitive; shared `auth.*` namespace)
| Key | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `jex.auth.secret` | HMAC secret (symmetric mode) |
| `jex.auth.jwksUri` | JWKS endpoint (asymmetric/IdP mode — use instead of secret) |
| `jex.auth.audience` / `jex.auth.issuer` / `jex.auth.algorithms` | Optional claim/algorithm restrictions |
| `jex.auth.exclude` | Comma-separated path prefixes to leave public |
| `jex.auth.required` | `true` = strict mode: unconfigured auth returns 503 instead of bypassing (recommended in production) |

⚠️ With neither `secret` nor `jwksUri` set, requests pass through (dev ergonomics; warning per request) — unless `jex.auth.required=true`, which fails loud with 503.

## What it unlocks (behavior lives in `models/`)
- Model `access` blocks become enforceable: `access: { read: "public", create: "user", update: "owner", delete: "admin" }` plus per-field rules.
- Verified JWT payload reaches handlers/hooks; `plugin-identity` requires this package to verify the tokens it issues.
- 401 = missing/malformed token; 403 = invalid/expired.
